name: Manav - Husky & Conversational Voice
description:
A naturally textured and approachable voice made for casual, real-life conversations and digital agents.

Manav’s tone is easygoing yet expressive—perfect for chatbots, AI companions, interactive learning bots, and customer care flows that need a human touch. His conversational Indian voice fits well across Replika, WhatsApp bots, IVRs, and platforms like YouTube, Spotify, and edtech apps where clarity meets warmth.

name: Beth - gentle and nurturing
description: A warm, comforting, motherly British English woman's voice, clear and easy to understand. Brilliant for use with children's stories and nursery rhymes. It's like having your very own Mary Poppins to read you bedtime books! It's beautiful for teaching, audiobook narration and social media such as YouTube, Tiktok, Reels and Stories. This studio-produced audio is also great for high-quality video dubbing, advertising and reading anything that will help you or your kids relax and unwind.

name: Kassandra Weiss - Alte Seherin & Orakel
description:
The voice of the Oracle - Kassandras voice carries the weight of an extraordinary life – sometimes warm and grandmotherly, then razor-sharp and bitingly sarcastic.
Perfect for mysterious mentor figures, urban fantasy, wise eccentrics with dark pasts, or any role that combines wisdom with world-weariness and unexpected truths with a touch of cynicism.

name: Daniel - Corporate Narration

It offers a trustworthy, human presence that makes complex content feel accessible and inviting.

name: Sandy - Deep, Slow and Soft
description:
Sandy – Deep, Slow-Burn Suspense Voice  - A deep, resonant voice with a slow and deliberate pace that builds tension with every word.

Sandy’s delivery style is perfect for chilling suspense, mystery thrillers, and atmospheric storytelling. Each pause and tone shift adds weight, making it ideal for slow-burn narratives.

Best suited for thriller podcasts, suspenseful audiobooks, dark fiction YouTube series, and crime mystery scripts on platforms like Audible, Pocket FM, Spotify, and YouTube Audio Drama.
